methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Extropic is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Guillaume Verdon, having raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private frontier-chip firms (including Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and Rain AI) and 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $50M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies $100M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage, same-sector private comparables yields a median of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring proved inapplicable due to absence of revenue disclosure for the target. 

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a range of $85M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $800M. The wide band reflects material dispersion within the private peer set, where valuations span an order of magnitude. Key uncertainties include Extropic's relative technical differentiation versus Etched and d-Matrix (valued at $5.0B and $2.0B respectively), capital efficiency, and go-to-market traction—factors not captured in stage and sector alone.
